economic_notes: Bonaparte First Nation has investments in 1049280 BC Ltd. and Bonaparte Sales Limited Partnership, accounted for using the modified equity method. Bonaparte Sales Limited Partnership had revenue of $393,750 and net earnings of $75,470 for the year.
notes: Audit opinion is qualified due to Social Housing tangible capital assets being amortized at a rate equivalent to annual principal reduction of related long-term debt (CMHC requirement) rather than over estimated useful lives as required by Canadian public sector accounting standards. The effect of this departure on net book value, amortization expense, annual surplus, and equity in tangible capital assets has not been determined. Two GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: 1049280 BC Ltd. and Bonaparte Sales Limited Partnership. ISC funding figure excludes the recovery of $77,116. Total government transfers calculated as ISC net ($3,968,506) + First Nations Health Authority ($736,290) + Government of Canada ($376,447) + CMHC ($122,387) + Province of BC ($1,020,083) + Other funding sources ($716,511) = $6,940,224 before netting ISC recovery; using gross ISC $4,045,622 + FNHA $736,290 + GOC $376,447 + CMHC $122,387 + BC $1,020,083 + Other funding $716,511 = $7,017,340 gross government transfers.
warnings: Qualified audit opinion issued — financial statements may not fully reflect true position
